body.title: [triage] Standalone rescueclaude uses inherited CC_TMUX_SESSION (cc_0) instead of _cc_RESCUE
body.intention: I intend that a standalone rescueclaude launch always names its session <hostname>_cc_RESCUE, honoring a superclaude-passed name only on a genuine delegation, so a stale inherited CC_TMUX_SESSION cannot mislabel a rescue session.
body.description:
BUG. Akien launches via `rescueclaude` (deliberately, to avoid tmux) expecting session `akiendelllinux_cc_RESCUE`, but gets `akiendelllinux_cc_0`. Root cause: `bin/rescueclaude:29` uses "is CC_TMUX_SESSION set?" as its proxy for "was I delegated by superclaude." That proxy is unreliable — a stale/inherited `CC_TMUX_SESSION=akiendelllinux_cc_0` in the environment (it survives in the parent shell; note `~/.bashrc:152` still carries a `#UUMOVED#`-commented `export CC_TMUX_SESSION=akiendelllinux_cc_0`) makes standalone rescueclaude take the delegated branch (_CC_DELEGATED=1) and skip its own `_cc_RESCUE` naming.

Proposed shape: make delegation EXPLICIT rather than inferred. superclaude sets a dedicated marker (e.g. `CC_LAUNCH_DELEGATED=1`) at the moment it execs/send-keys rescueclaude; rescueclaude treats a bare inherited `CC_TMUX_SESSION` WITHOUT that marker as standalone and derives `<hostname>_cc_RESCUE`. Also scrub the stale source so it stops leaking: remove the dead `#UUMOVED#` line at `~/.bashrc:152` (env hygiene, out of the repo — call out to Akien, don't silently edit).

**Affected files:** bin/rescueclaude (delegation detection at line 29-34), bin/superclaude (set the explicit delegation marker before handing off, near lines 326/341/353/359), tests/test_cc_session_naming.py (or a new test targeting the launcher naming logic). Out-of-repo: ~/.bashrc:152 stale export (flag to Akien).
**Design rules:** ticket_design_checks (test-plan-or-why-not applies). no-sqlite N/A. no-escape-hatch: fix the detection root cause, don't add a rescue-only override flag that papers over it.
**Scope boundary:** IN — explicit delegation signal from superclaude + rescueclaude honoring it (marker present -> honor passed name; marker absent -> _cc_RESCUE regardless of inherited CC_TMUX_SESSION); the naming test. OUT — the reload-harness fragility already tracked in T-cc-session-naming-reload-harness-fragility; the Windows variants in T-launcher-windows-variants-delegate-to-rescue; any tmux-orchestration change.
**Test plan:** proof-on-close. A test invokes rescueclaude's session-name resolution with (a) CC_TMUX_SESSION set but NO delegation marker -> asserts the resolved name ends in `_cc_RESCUE`; (b) CC_TMUX_SESSION set WITH the marker -> asserts it honors the passed name. Case (a) is RED on today's code (returns the inherited cc_0 name) and GREEN after.
**Proof obligation:** a hollow build cannot pass case (a) — inheriting CC_TMUX_SESSION without a genuine delegation must NOT hijack the rescue session name; the standalone path must produce `_cc_RESCUE`. This discharges the sub-intention that delegation be a signal superclaude actually sends, not an ambient env var any prior launch can leave behind.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
**Completion criteria:** standalone `rescueclaude` (no delegation marker, even with a stale CC_TMUX_SESSION in env) produces `<hostname>_cc_RESCUE`; a genuine superclaude delegation still honors the passed `hostname_cc_N` name; the naming test passes (case (a) red-before/green-after).
